title: psephellus geghamensis
intro: Psephellus geghamensis is a flowering plant species in the dandelion tribe of the sunflower family, native to Armenia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant properties. Psephellus geghamensis is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal effects.
